mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales captures underlying consumer demand strength; higher demand directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS coincide with negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year rolling window, or the strategy's Sharpe ratio is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, elevated joblessness signals weak household income formation and falling aggregate demand, which pressures corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8 percent, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1 percent and industrial production simultaneously rises, ultra-loose monetary policy amplifies a manufacturing rebound, boosting corporate profit expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of FEDFUNDS below 1 percent and rising INDPRO fail to produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over single-series models in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate aftermath of a recession, an increase in quarterly real GDP signals a turn in aggregate demand that is not yet fully priced into equities, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: During post-recession quarters, positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the strategy underperforms buy-and-hold by more than 500 basis points annualized over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing output and supply-side stress, which reduces near-term earnings visibility and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the mean forward return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5 percent signals maximum monetary accommodation that compresses short-term risk-free rates and pushes capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Instances of FEDFUNDS below 0.5 percent fail to generate positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 50 percent over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirm a synchronized recovery in both labor income and consumer spending, which drives a sustained bid in equity prices and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and RSAFS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint upward moves in PAYEMS and RSAFS coincide with negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or a model including the interaction term does not improve out-of-sample R-squared relative to an additive model (delta R-sq < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 600 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, which drags the S&P 500 lower over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST below 600 thousand is followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the cumulative return in triggered episodes is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).
